ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Continued To Aug-27-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Kithas for and with John M. Gregson, Trustee (via Zoom).
Theodore Bacon for Anne-Marie O'Halloran, Beneficiary (via Zoom).

The Court confirmed that the issue related to jewelry had been resolved per the parties' prior settlement.

The discussion then turned to the appropriate procedure for resolving the outstanding fee and surcharge matters. The Court noted the extensive history of the litigation, the detailed filings already submitted by the parties, and the need to minimize further depletion of trust assets. The Court weighed the options of holding an evidentiary hearing, proceeding by written submissions, or returning the parties to mediation.

Mr. Kithas and Mr. Bacon each addressed their clients' positions. Mr. Kithas initially indicated willingness to submit the matter on the papers but later requested an evidentiary hearing. The Court expressed concern about the inconsistency and emphasized the need to proceed efficiently. Mr. Bacon opposed further evidentiary proceedings and proposed limited briefing or argument as alternatives.

Following further discussion, the Court ordered the parties to return to mediation in light of the continuing disputes, the potential for resolution, and the cost of further litigation. The Court ordered the parties to complete mediation by July 30, 2025, and set a hearing on status of mediation for August 27, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. All underlying petitions were also continued to the same date and time.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Trustee John M Gregsons First Petition For Fees
Off Calendar Apr-23-2025 Continued To Jun-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Theodore Bacon for Anne-Marie O'Halloran, Beneficiary (via Zoom).
John A. Kithas for and with John M. Gregson, Trustee (via Zoom).
Anne-Marie O'Halloran was present in the courtroom.

The Court and parties discussed how best to move forward with the multiple overlapping issues. Mr. Kithas proposed that all related matters - including the fee petitions, surcharge requests, and sanctions - be coordinated, with filings due by May 16, 2025. He also proposed limited depositions and a hearing in mid-July. Mr. Bacon agreed that the issues should be streamlined but opposed further depositions and asked that proceedings be kept simple to avoid more costs to the trust. The Court emphasized the importance of limiting further expenses and expressed skepticism regarding the likelihood of success on either party's surcharge claims.

The Court urged both sides to think carefully about whether the additional discovery and filings would be worth the remaining trust funds, which are limited. The Court encouraged cooperation to simplify the remaining issues for hearing.

After discussion, the court continued the matter to June 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and set a Case Management Conference for hearing on the same date and time. Mr. Kithas was directed to file the petition for attorney's fees by May 16, 2025. Counsel were ordered to file a joint case management statement by June 12, 2025, summarizing what issues remain, what discovery (if any) is still needed, and what has been resolved.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
